# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 327
- **Lines of code**: 63,488
- **Languages**: TypeScript (259), JSON (22), CSS (14), Other (10), Markdown (10)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-04T21:39:08.742Z

## Architecture Zones

### Hench Agent Core (`hench-agent-core`)

Agent orchestration layer: task briefing, multi-turn LLM loop, completion validation, review flow, and run persistence.

- Files: 49
- Cohesion: 0.97, Coupling: 0.03
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/index.ts, packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts

### Hench Agent Tooling (`hench-agent-tooling`)

Sandboxed tool implementations (file I/O, shell, git) and security guard subsystem that validates every agent action.

- Files: 12
- Cohesion: 0.84, Coupling: 0.16
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/tools.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts

### Rex Analysis Pipeline (`rex-analysis`)

Project scanning, deduplication, LLM-enriched proposal generation, and conflict reconciliation pipeline for PRD item discovery.

- Files: 17
- Cohesion: 0.77, Coupling: 0.23
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/analyze/index.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts

### Rex PRD Core (`rex-prd-core`)

PRD data model, tree operations, storage adapters (file and Notion), state machine, sync engine, schema validation, and all non-analyze CLI commands.

- Files: 78
- Cohesion: 0.97, Coupling: 0.03
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/cli/commands/constants.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/errors.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts, packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts, packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts (+2 more)

### Root Orchestration (`root-orchestration`)

Top-level CLI dispatcher, CI pipeline runner, and unified configuration manager for the n-dx toolkit.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00

### Sourcevision Analysis Engine (`sourcevision-analysis-engine`)

Core static analysis pipeline: file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain zone detection, React component cataloging, AI enrichment, and output generation (CONTEXT.md, llms.txt).

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.97, Coupling: 0.03
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/next-steps.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts

### Sourcevision Test Fixture (`sourcevision-test-fixture`)

Minimal TypeScript project (7 files) used as a deterministic integration test baseline for validating the analysis pipeline produces correct inventory, import graphs, and zone output.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00

### Sourcevision Viewer (`sourcevision-viewer`)

Preact-based interactive web UI with force-directed graph visualization, zone/file browsers, architecture views, and dual-mode data loading (dev server or drag-and-drop static files).

- Files: 49
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts` (73 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts` (26 imports)
- `packages/sourcevision/src/schema/index.ts` (22 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/store/index.ts` (20 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (18 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/errors.ts` (17 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts` (17 imports)
- `packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts` (16 imports)
- `packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts` (15 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/core/canonical.ts` (14 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 108 files)
- `preact` (used by 23 files)
- `rex` (used by 10 files)
- `zod` (used by 6 files)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (used by 3 files)
- `typescript` (used by 3 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `child_process` (used by 2 files)
- `path` (used by 2 files)
- `url` (used by 2 files)

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Patterns

- All cross-package data contracts (sourcevision JSON files, rex PRDStore interface, rex schema types) flow in a single direction matching the execution pipeline: sourcevision produces → rex consumes and produces → hench consumes — no package produces data consumed by an upstream package. [info]
- All three packages converge on the same schema/validate.ts + schema/v1.ts + cli/errors.ts structural pattern, establishing a de facto package template without a shared generator or scaffolding tool [info]
- Duplicated project-config merge logic in rex/src/store/project-config.ts and hench/src/store/project-config.ts performs identical .n-dx.json loading and deep-merge — extractable to a shared workspace package [info]
- The three integration mechanisms (subprocess, filesystem JSON, library import) form a coupling gradient that mirrors package stability: most-stable sourcevision uses loosest coupling (data files), moderately-stable rex uses medium coupling (subprocess + data), and the tightest coupling (library imports) exists only between the two most co-evolved packages (hench→rex). [info]
- The PRDStore interface from rex/dist/store/types.js is threaded through brief.ts, loop.ts, cli-loop.ts, and tools/rex.ts as a constructor parameter, acting as the primary dependency-inversion boundary between hench and rex. [info] (hench-agent-core)
- All tool implementations (files.ts, git.ts, shell.ts) are stateless functions with identical signatures (params + guard → result), but rex.ts breaks this pattern by also importing agent-layer logic (validateCompletion, formatValidationResult). [info] (hench-agent-tooling)
- scanners.ts duplicates sourcevision type definitions locally instead of sharing them, creating a shadow type contract that could silently drift if sourcevision's JSON schema evolves. [warning] (rex-analysis)
- The smart-add.ts command imports from the analyze zone, creating a second consumer of the analysis pipeline beyond the primary analyze.ts command — this prevents the analyze zone from being treated as a single-consumer internal module. [info] (rex-prd-core)
- Three files use three different integration mechanisms with sub-packages: cli.js spawns with inherited stdio, ci.js spawns and parses JSON stdout, config.js does direct filesystem I/O — no shared abstraction for package communication. [info] (root-orchestration)
- next-steps.ts lives inside analyzers/ but produces output consumed exclusively by the viewer — it is functionally a boundary module misplaced in the producer zone [info] (sourcevision-analysis-engine)
- ... and 2 more

### Relationships

- Hench→Rex runtime coupling (9 function imports across 6 files) is the sole cross-package dependency — if rex core APIs change, hench breaks with no compile-time safety beyond TypeScript path resolution against dist/ [warning]
- Rex has no explicit public API module (index.ts barrel) despite being consumed as a library by hench — hench reaches into rex/dist/store/types, rex/dist/core/*, and rex/dist/schema/* directly, creating a fragile integration surface. [warning]
- Rex's shadow type definitions for sourcevision data (SVFinding, SVZone, etc. in scanners.ts) and the absence of a shared schema package mean the sourcevision↔rex data contract has no compile-time enforcement and could drift silently. [warning]
- Hench imports rex core utilities (findNextTask, findItem, walkTree, computeTimestampUpdates, findAutoCompletions) that are implementation details rather than public API — these are internal algorithms that could change without notice. [warning] (hench-agent-core)
- tools/rex.ts importing agent/completion.ts is the sole cause of bidirectional coupling between hench zones — extracting validateCompletion into a shared module or the schema layer would make the dependency unidirectional. [warning] (hench-agent-tooling)
- The analysis pipeline imports only 3 symbols from rex-prd-core (PRDItem type, Priority type, walkTree function), while the reverse direction (CLI commands importing analyze) pulls in ~15 symbols — the dependency is heavily asymmetric despite being bidirectional. [info] (rex-analysis)
- Hench imports 5+ internal rex modules via rex/dist/* paths with no public API barrel file — any internal refactoring in rex could break hench without warning since there is no stable API boundary. [warning] (rex-prd-core)
- config.js must understand the JSON schema of every package's config file, creating implicit coupling to all three packages' internal config formats without compile-time safety. [warning] (root-orchestration)

### Observations

- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-agent-core" ↔ "hench-agent-tooling" (4+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "rex-analysis" ↔ "rex-prd-core" (14+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "sourcevision-analysis-engine" ↔ "sourcevision-viewer" (3+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 78 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition [info]
- High cohesion (0.97) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-agent-core)
- High cohesion (0.84) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-agent-tooling)
- High cohesion (0.97) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (rex-prd-core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (root-orchestration)
- High cohesion (0.97) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (sourcevision-analysis-engine)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (sourcevision-test-fixture)
- ... and 30 more
